  "findings": [
    "Sling angle is below the recommended minimum of 30 degrees",
    "Tension in each sling leg (7,099 lb) exceeds the tagged Working Load Limit (7,000 lb)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current rigging plan",
    "Increase the sling angle to at least 45 or 60 degrees by using longer slings",
    "Ensure the tension in each leg is well below the rated Working Load Limit"
  ],
  "rationale": "The tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle) = (6,000 / 2) / sin(25°) = 3,000 / 0.4226 = 7,098.6 lb. This exceeds the 7,000 lb WLL of each leg. Additionally, ASME B30.9 and OSHA standards strongly discourage or prohibit sling angles below 30 degrees due to the rapid increase in tension and instability.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
